chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: He did not see that one coming.

chat.author: Reshad Rahman
chat.text:
<with apologies to my Italian friends>
Did Paolo see Italy at the WC?

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: No ad hominem, but apparently today ad oculum

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: BMP Maintenance Padawans

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Next usual step is write down some guidelines in the wiki

chat.author: Luuk Hendriks
chat.text: re slides, I have submitted an updated set which have not yet made it through it seems. Did I mess up somewhere perhaps?

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: let me check

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: OK - should be approved, thanks for the nudge

chat.author: Luuk Hendriks
chat.text: thanks!

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: The opposite side of the argument where we delete it is we're left with md5 (which is supported?) and we're told "don't use that!"

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: i will defend the reality of the situation

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Reviewing RFC 7950, the grammar says "if-feature" is fine for choice, so that's a fine answer.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: +1 to Thomas to do BMP-v5, TLVs all the way.  Goes along with the design criteria discussion.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: A chunk of the design criteria is "how do we avoid needing to stall things for incremental deployment for a new feature".  BGP is mostly resilient because it's TLVs in the important parts.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @jeff, not all implementations will support tcp-ao. Therefore the bmp yang module needs to accomondate that with a YANG feature statement. I believe that from a YANG modeling perspective, the TCP-AO specification is stable and won't change. Therefore we should implement it in this document with a normative reference but not delay the publication process. IETF has forseen such situations and has designed a process for that. In that very unlikely case where the TCP-AO specification would change and has impact on the YANG modeling, we still can do a bis and new YANG module revision.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @jeff, a deviations you would do if a certain part of the YANG module which was intended a design time of the module is not implemented. Since TCP-AO is optional, a feature is the right choice here.

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @paolo and @jeff, I fear that we will have multiple BMPv4 implementations we have to deal with in deployments. Therefore, I believe major changes demand a revision change. Hence the proposal to wrap-up with BMPv4 in its current state and move forward with BMPv5 and give the design team the flexibility to change anything they want.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: I'm highly supportive of version bumps for major pdu changes.  Success is we stop bumping version because the extension mechanism no longer requires it.  BGP has been version 4 for quite a while. :-)

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: An interesting observation is the if-feature for tcp-ao likely should carry into the bgp model.  Time for an issue in github. :-/

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: https://github.com/mjethanandani/ietf-bgp-yang/issues/500

chat.author: Thomas Graf
chat.text: @jeff and @prasad, since most of the presented work is addressing scale, I would like to see a design team where the current BMP implementers are collaborating and reach consensus wherever they proposal help their implementations to scale.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: This is obvious?

chat.author: Paolo Lucente
chat.text: Thanks Jeff and Thomas for your feedback and steering towards BMPv5 for new design stuff and wrapping up BMPv4

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: :sparkling_heart: for the RPKI directory info

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: The mapping table for country often uses the ISO country code table.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: The AS numbers to names would be nice to grab from canonical sources. I've not done it in a while, but have previously used maxmind

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: and while AS seems orthogonal for the embedded AS options it'd be nice.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: How many floppy discs does it take for the snapshot?

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: @Jeffrey Haas AS-2-name can be done via Team Cymru

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: (or RDAP)

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Right. The doc should (SHOULD?) include a recommended mapping mechanism.

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: does it really? do all docs that mention ASes have such a mapping reference? :-P

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Docs are mostly not discussing how to generate canonicalized string expansions for operational purposes.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text:
For the minutes, my faulty memory about where RS was standardized was wrong.  RS core spec was IDR, this was grow:
https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc7948/

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: Yeah the RS story ended up split like that after multiple rounds of lengthy discussions :)

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: "hot potato" - "not mine!"

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: @Jeffrey Haas mapping CDR ASN to "text" is a bit hairy... because (apologies for being PKI-pedantic here) There is no explicit authentication associated with a CDR, since the PKI used for CDR validation provides authorization but not authentication. Although the CDR is a signed, application-layer object, there is no intent to convey non-repudiation via a CDR. _In other words: clients know the CDR was validly signed but not by which real-world entity_

chat.author: Pierre Francois
chat.text: :-D. Might be worth presenting this at rtgwg.

chat.author: Martin Pels
chat.text:
For the mapping initially we were thinking to initially do self-contained (within the same JSON) and IANA.
Mapping pointers to other sources seems like a never-ending project.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Job/Martin: The point is "what do you display for X field".  The spec is "this is what AS X says they want their communities to mean". Great!  But if you want similar expansion for types that are embedded ASes, the recursive expansion of the AS name next to the number will want something.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: And perhaps the answer is simply "the AS map is left to the router to figure out"

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: I mention maxmind partially because it's shipping code for our SRX platform.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: For this BMP RPKI talk note that the SIDROPs discussion about RPKI data source priority will apply.

chat.author: Martin Pels
chat.text: Jeff: I see the value, but maxmind is not an authoritative source :)

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: I make no argument that it is, simply offer it as an example of how one does such resolution.  Pointers to canonical registries would be better.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: Sadly IANA basically says "see RDAP".

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: The "RPKI impact" is nice per-prefix meta-data.  However, tracking impact may not be available in an implementation.  E.g. "localpref is changed" may be hard to track without wasting memory.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: For the minutes, "what do we do about origin?"

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: :skull_and_crossbones::skull: are rather tempting for some op events...

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: To Prasad's mic point for Jing Zhao and Thomas' point - most of what we need is to make sure our data model generically permits tagging of things at the right level of hierarchy while being mindful to not create state that impacts route scaling.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text:
For PAVA, a slight tangent, but see also DANE:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DNS-based_Authentication_of_Named_Entities

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: In that case, certs. But since we're talking RPKI - information in DNS protected by DNSSEC.

chat.author: Jeffrey Haas
chat.text: DNSBL for BGP. :-)
